metadata.title: Belgium EOR Services - Employer of Record Belgium
metadata.description: Belgium EOR and Employer of Record Belgium services. Hire employees in Belgium without entity. Belgian employment law 2026, ONSS/RSZ payroll, visa support. Onboard in 2–3 days. Book a demo.
keywords: hire employees in Belgium without entity

faqs.title: Belgium EOR FAQs
faqs.subtitle: Quick answers on cost, timeline, legal risks, and contracts for Employer of Record in Belgium.

items.question: How much does Belgium EOR cost?
items.answer: Belgium EOR pricing typically includes a monthly management fee per employee plus the cost of salary, benefits, employer ONSS/RSZ contributions, and taxes. At Jackson & Frank we offer transparent pricing with no hidden fees. Total cost depends on headcount, salary levels, and benefits. Book a demo for a custom quote.

items.question: How fast can I hire someone in Belgium with EOR?
items.answer: With Jackson & Frank Belgium EOR, you can onboard new hires in 2–3 business days once we have signed contracts and employee details. No local entity (BV/SRL) is required, so you avoid months of setup time.

items.question: What are the legal risks of hiring in Belgium without an entity?
items.answer: When you use a compliant Belgium EOR like Jackson & Frank, the EOR is the legal employer in Belgium. They assume employment law, ONSS/RSZ, tax, and compliance risk. You avoid misclassification, permanent establishment, and local registration issues as long as you work with a licensed, established provider.

items.question: What type of employment contract does Belgium EOR use?
items.answer: Employees are hired under a Belgian employment contract (arbeidsovereenkomst/contrat de travail) compliant with Belgian law notice periods, joint committee (paritair comité), holiday pay, 13th month, and mandatory benefits. Jackson & Frank issues the contract from our Belgian entity so it is fully local and enforceable.

items.question: Can you sponsor work permits for Belgium EOR employees?
items.answer: Yes. We support work permits (type A, B, or C) and EU Blue Card for highly skilled workers in Belgium, plus coordination with VDAB, Actiris, Forem, and immigration authorities so you can hire non-EU talent through our Belgium EOR.
